preamble: Spirantization of the voiced aspirated dental stop [dʱ] into the voiced dental fricative [ð] is a significant shift in historical linguistics, frequently appearing in the development of Greek and Germanic consonant systems.

examples.note: δ (delta) was originally a voiced stop [d] or possibly reflected aspirated stages in certain environments before becoming [ð].
Proto-Germanic voiced stops were often fricatives [β, ð, ɣ] medially, originating from PIE aspirated stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
